name: Shrimp in Bamboo Shape
recipeInstructions:
1. Peel and devein the shrimp, leaving the last tail segment intact. Rinse the shrimp and pat dry. Make a small crosswise incision on each shrimp.
2. Sprinkle the shrimp with 1 teaspoon salt and sesame oil, then rub it in evenly.
3. Dip each shrimp individually into the cornstarch.
4. In a separate bowl, mix half a cup of remaining cornstarch, 1 egg, and the leftover salt until well combined.
5. Heat the peanut oil for deep frying in a wok until hot and almost smoking. Carefully dip each shrimp into the egg-cornstarch mixture, then gently slide them into the hot oil. Cook, turning occasionally, until golden brown all over, about 2 minutes. Remove the shrimp and drain on paper towels.
6. Return all the shrimp to the hot oil and deep fry for an additional minute. Drain again and serve immediately.
